nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
### **How to Use This Workflow:**
📝 **YouTube Comment Sentiment Analyzer**

1. 🔘 **Trigger:** Click "Execute Workflow" to run it manually.

2. 📄 Your Google Sheet should have **2 sheets**:
   - **Sheet1 (Results with Sentiment):**
     - Column A: `commentId` (YouTube comment id)
     - Column B: `video_url` (url of video)
     - Column C: `comment` (YouTube comment)
     - Column D: `authorName` (Name of author as per Youtube)
     - Column E: `likes` (Number of likes on that particular comment)
     - Column f: `reply` (Number of replies on that particular comment)
     - Column g: `sentiment` (Analyzed sentiment of the comment)
     - Column h: `published_at` (timestamp of comment published)
   
   - **Sheet2 (Video URLs):**
     - Column A: `video_urls` (list of YouTube video URLs)
     - Column B: `last_fetched_time` (timestamp of the last fetch)
     - Column C: `next_fetch_time` (time for the next fetch)

3. 🔐 **Make sure these credentials are set up**:
   - Google Sheets (Service Account)
   - YouTube Data API v3
   - OpenAI API Key (for sentiment analysis)

4. ✅ **What this workflow does**:
   - Reads **video URLs** from **Sheet2**.
   - Checks **last fetched time** (if applicable).
   - Fetches new comments from YouTube.
   - Analyzes sentiment using OpenAI.
   - Appends **comment**, **sentiment**, **video ID**, and **timestamp** to **Sheet1**.
   - Updates **last_fetched** timestamp in **Sheet2**.

5. 💡 **Tip:**
   - You can replace the **Manual Trigger** with a **Cron node** for automatic execution at specified intervals.

parameters.content: # 🚀 YouTube Comment Sentiment Analyzer with Google Sheets & OpenAI

### **How to Use This Workflow:**
📝 **YouTube Comment Sentiment Analyzer**

1. 🔘 **Trigger:** Click "Execute Workflow" to run it manually.

2. 📄 Your Google Sheet should have **2 sheets**:
   - **Sheet1 (Results with Sentiment):**
     - Column A: `commentId` (YouTube comment id)
     - Column B: `video_url` (url of video)
     - Column C: `comment` (YouTube comment)
     - Column D: `authorName` (Name of author as per Youtube)
     - Column E: `likes` (Number of likes on that particular comment)
     - Column f: `reply` (Number of replies on that particular comment)
     - Column g: `sentiment` (Analyzed sentiment of the comment)
     - Column h: `published_at` (timestamp of comment published)
   
   - **Sheet2 (Video URLs):**
     - Column A: `video_urls` (list of YouTube video URLs)
     - Column B: `last_fetched_time` (timestamp of the last fetch)
     - Column C: `next_fetch_time` (time for the next fetch)

3. 🔐 **Make sure these credentials are set up**:
   - Google Sheets (Service Account)
   - YouTube Data API v3
   - OpenAI API Key (for sentiment analysis)

4. ✅ **What this workflow does**:
   - Reads **video URLs** from **Sheet2**.
   - Checks **last fetched time** (if applicable).
   - Fetches new comments from YouTube.
   - Analyzes sentiment using OpenAI.
   - Appends **comment**, **sentiment**, **video ID**, and **timestamp** to **Sheet1**.
   - Updates **last_fetched** timestamp in **Sheet2**.

5. 💡 **Tip:**
   - You can replace the **Manual Trigger** with a **Cron node** for automatic execution at specified intervals.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the "Split Out" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "No Operation, do nothing" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Video Urls from Google Sheet" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "check next fetch time is available or not" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "check next fetch time is before the current time" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Comments for video urls" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Analyze sentiment of every comment" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Insert and update comment in google sheet" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Update last fetched time and next_fetch_time" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "No Operation, do nothing1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Check Success Response" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Format fields as required to save in google sheet".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
